title: Montgomery's Peggy.
content:
Tune - "Galla-Water."


I.

        Altho' my bed were in yon muir,
            Amang the heather, in my plaidie,
        Yet happy, happy would I be,
            Had I my dear Montgomery's Peggy.

II.

        When o'er the hill beat surly storms,
            And winter nights were dark and rainy;
        I'd seek some dell, and in my arms
            I'd shelter dear Montgomery's Peggy.

III.

        Were I a baron proud and high,
            And horse and servants waiting ready,
        Then a' 'twad gie o' joy to me,
            The sharin't with Montgomery's Peggy.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Montgomery's Peggy.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
